Phase-32.NAME: Domain TLS and Shared 443 Edge
Phase-32.Goal: Define and implement the krotpn.xyz production edge for HTTPS redirect, wildcard TLS, and shared 443 MTProto coexistence.

NonGoals.item-1: No redesign of operator-approved deploy/install scripts without explicit approval.
NonGoals.item-2: No admin UX work in this phase.

Steps.step-1: Define edge settings for krotpn.xyz, canonical frontend URL, wildcard certificate paths, and MTProto SNI domain suffix.
Steps.step-2: Add static nginx/edge contract for HTTP to HTTPS redirect and canonical host handling.
Steps.step-3: Design shared 443 routing: MTProto fake-TLS by issued SNI, normal HTTPS fallback to KrotPN nginx/frontend/backend/admin.
Steps.step-4: Create local/static verification for redirect, certificate path expectations, and no accidental deploy/install rewrite.
Steps.step-5: Prepare rollback notes and staging/live smoke checklist for krotpn.xyz cutover.
Steps.step-6: Run production-like smoke only after operator provides wildcard certificate and DNS readiness.

Verification.command: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_domain_tls_edge_static.py -v
live smoke: http://krotpn.xyz redirects to https://krotpn.xyz and HTTPS surfaces stay healthy

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: HTTP requests to krotpn.xyz redirect permanently to HTTPS.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Wildcard certificate covers krotpn.xyz and *.krotpn.xyz before MTProto production links are advertised.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Shared 443 edge is tested with both normal HTTPS and at least one issued MTProto SNI.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Rollback path restores the previous nginx-only 443 behavior if shared edge fails.

Risks.risk-1: 443 ownership conflict between nginx and MTProto edge can take the whole public surface down.
Risks.risk-2: Let's Encrypt wildcard DNS-01 is operator-dependent and cannot be fully proven locally.
